fact: May 29 is exactly 149 days into the year, which means there are only 216 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby rabbits to be hopping around everywhere - they're about 3-4 weeks old and super fluffy!
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern cities by this date, making it one of the earliest sunrise times of the year.
Dandelions are going completely bonkers right now - their white puffy seed heads are ready to explode and fly everywhere when you blow on them!
May 29 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposed to be extra chatty and curious.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant orange and red.
Fireflies start blinking their butts like crazy around this time in many parts of North America - it's their peak mating season!
The moon can look extra huge and orange when it rises on late May evenings because of all the pollen and dust particles floating in the warm air.
Cherry trees are dropping their petals like pink snow in many places, creating natural confetti carpets on sidewalks and streets.
This date is exactly one month before the summer solstice, so daylight is stretching longer every single day in the north.
Tadpoles in ponds are starting to sprout tiny legs around this time - they look like weird little aliens with tails!
The air temperature and ground temperature are almost perfectly matched on May 29 in many temperate regions, creating ideal conditions for outdoor picnics.
